findings.description: The North Carolina Marine Fisheries logo/seal appears in a slightly different rendering between PDF and HTML, but is present and correctly placed in both.
findings.evidence: PDF shows the seal with '200 years' celebrating badge; HTML shows the same seal in the same location.

findings.description: Page 2 in the HTML has excessive whitespace/blank area in the middle of the page, with the footer appearing mid-page and then the content from page 2 of the PDF appearing below it, creating a disjointed layout.
findings.evidence: PDF page 2 flows directly from page 1 content (trawl restrictions continuing into General Information section). HTML page 2 shows a large blank gap with a repeated footer block mid-page before continuing the content.

findings.description: The HTML rendering of pages 2-3 shows content from the end of page 1 (Section II.E, III gear restrictions) repeated or repositioned with a large gap and footer interruption, disrupting the natural reading flow.
findings.evidence: PDF pages flow smoothly from gear restrictions to general information. HTML has footer blocks and blank space interrupting the flow between these sections.

findings.description: The signature of Kathy B. Rawls appears in the PDF as a handwritten signature image but appears to be missing/not rendered in the HTML version on the final page.
findings.evidence: PDF page 5 shows a handwritten signature above 'Kathy B. Rawls, Director'. HTML final page shows the signature line but the handwritten signature image appears absent.

findings.description: The HTML rendering appears to show the document content twice - once as a PDF-like rendering and then again as a reformatted HTML version below, creating duplicate content.
findings.evidence: HTML pages show the same proclamation content repeated - first in a format closer to the original PDF layout, then again in a reflowed HTML format with different styling.

findings.description: The document uses only h1 for the title 'PROCLAMATION' but all major section headings (I. SIZE LIMIT, II. HARVEST PERIOD AND LIMITS, III. GEAR RESTRICTIONS, IV. GENERAL INFORMATION) are rendered as list items with bold/underline formatting rather than as proper h2 or h3 headings. This is a structural choice but means there's no heading hierarchy beyond h1.
findings.evidence:
PDF sections I through IV are distinct structural sections. In HTML they appear as
  • SIZE LIMIT etc. within an
      rather than as headings.

findings.description: The alt text for the second embedded image is empty, and the third image's alt text references a signature that may be speculative.
findings.evidence: Second image: alt="". Third image: alt="Composite layered image including signature of Kathy B. Rawls" — the PDF page 3 does contain Kathy B. Rawls' signature, so this is reasonably accurate.

findings.description: The page headers/footers and the state seal/logo image are repeated three times in the HTML, corresponding to each PDF page. While this mirrors the PDF's per-page headers/footers, it creates visual duplication in a continuous HTML document, particularly the footer appearing mid-content.
findings.evidence: The footer 'State of North Carolina | Division of Marine Fisheries...' appears three times, and multiple page header images are embedded within the flow of the list content.
